mission.title: containerd: [Go 1.24] v2.2.0 fails to create containers from images having /etc/{passwd,group} symlinked to an absolute path
mission.description: [Go 1.24] v2.2.0 fails to create containers from images having /etc/{passwd,group} symlinked to an absolute path. This issue affects 17+ users.

steps.title: Identify containerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your containerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
Container creation with containerd 2.2.0 fails with `path escapes from parent` errors for images that contain `/etc/passwd` and `/etc/group` as absolute symlinks.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Go 1.24] v2.2.0 fails to create containers from images…
steps.description:
I introduced a helper function `openUserFile` in `pkg/oci/spec_opts.go` to wrap the file opening logic for `UserFromFS` and `GIDFromFS`.

The logic is as follows:
1. Attempt to open the file normally.
2. If `Open` fails, check if the filesystem supports `ReadLink` (using a local interface
```yaml
### Any other relevant information

containerd 2.2.0 is built with go 1.24.3[^2] whereas containerd 1.7.28 is built with go 1.23.0[^3].
go 1.24 introduces a check[^4] that may produce the "path escapes from parent" error[^5] that I've experienced.

[^2]: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/blob/v2.2.0/go.mod#L3
[^3]: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/blob/v2.1.5/go.mod#L3
[^4]: https://github.com/golang/go/commit/43d90c6a14e7b3fd1b3b8085b8071a09231c4b62#diff-47957b402486ac2a9d4182ae5fa01371df1eb7abab86e1c543b5d1af3fc4deccR15
[^5]: https://github.com/golang/go/commit/43d90c6a14e7b3f
```

steps.title: Upgrade containerd to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade containerd:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade containerd containerd/containerd --namespace containerd
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd
helm list -n containerd
```

steps.title: Confirm [Go 1.24] v2.2.0 fails to create containers from… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
I introduced a helper function `openUserFile` in `pkg/oci/spec_opts.go` to wrap the file opening logic for `UserFromFS` and `GIDFromFS`.

The logic is as follows:
1. Attempt to open the file normally.
2. If `Open` fails, check if the filesystem supports `ReadLink` (using a local interface `readLinker` to maintain compatibility with Go versions prior to 1.23).
3.
